title: From 'Starfield' to Artemis: designing moonbases in games and reality
summary: The article compares lunar settlement designs in video games like 'Starfield' with NASA's real-life plans for Artemis Base Camp. It explores how gaming concepts align or differ from actual space agency strategies.

title: Watch Rocket Lab launch Japanese 'origami' satellite, 7 other spacecraft to orbit tonight
summary: Rocket Lab is set to launch eight Japanese satellites to orbit, including one with an origami-folded antenna. The launch is scheduled for April 22 and will be livestreamed.

title: NASA still confident that Artemis astronauts will land on the moon in 2028 despite spacesuit delays
summary: NASA remains confident that Artemis astronauts will land on the Moon by 2028 despite concerns over spacesuit development delays highlighted in a new report.

title: Happy Earth Day! Celebrate with these amazing photos of our planet from space, from Apollo 8 to Artemis 2
summary: Earth Day is celebrated with historical photos of Earth from space, spanning missions from Apollo 8 to Artemis 2. The event highlights humanity's perspective on our planet over time.

title: Meet the US Navy divers who welcomed the Artemis 2 astronauts home from the moon
summary: Four US Navy divers played a key role in the Artemis 2 moon mission by recovering the astronauts upon their return to Earth and ensuring their safety and health. Their involvement highlights the interagency collaboration essential for NASA's lunar exploration efforts.

title: Blue Origin's huge New Glenn rocket grounded after launch mishap
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket failed to deliver a satellite to the proper orbit during its third launch, leading to a temporary grounding by the FAA. The incident marks another setback for the company's ambitious spaceflight ambitions.

title: Celebrate Earth Day with this Artemis 2 shot | Space photo of the day for April 22, 2026
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission captured an image of Earth to celebrate Earth Day. The photo was featured as the Space Photo of the Day for April 22, 2026.

title: Spectacular photos of the 2026 Lyrid meteor shower captured from Earth and space
summary: Spectacular images of the 2026 Lyrid meteor shower were captured from Earth and space, showing meteors blazing through Earth's atmosphere around the April 22 peak.

title: NASA SBIR/STTR Phase I and II BAA, 2026 Appendix A and B are now live! Offers due May 21, 2026, 5:00PM EDT
summary: NASA has opened its SBIR/STTR Phase I and II BAA for 2026, with proposals due by May 21, 2026. The program aims to fund innovative research and development projects from small businesses and research institutions.

title: NASA’s 777 Aircraft Returns Home with Science Flights on the Horizon
summary: NASA's Boeing 777 has returned to its fleet after structural modifications to become an airborne science laboratory. The aircraft arrived at NASA's Langley Research Center in Virginia after a check flight and transit from Waco.

title: NASA Targets Early September for Roman Space Telescope Launch
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope is now targeting an early September 2026 launch, ahead of the previously committed deadline of May 2027. The accelerated development is attributed to collaboration between public investment, institutional expertise, and private enterprise.

title: NASA Astronaut Anil Menon to Discuss Upcoming Launch, Mission
summary: NASA will hold a news conference to discuss astronaut Anil Menon's upcoming mission to the International Space Station. The event will be livestreamed on NASA's YouTube channel.

title: Night and (Earth) Day
summary: NASA released an Earth Day image showing the terminator line between night and day, captured by Artemis II astronauts during their lunar mission in 2026.

title: Advancing Earth Observation at NASA Since Release of Earthrise Photo
summary: NASA has significantly advanced Earth observation technology since the iconic Earthrise photo in 1968, evolving from simple spacecraft window cameras to powerful radar systems.

title: Artemis II Mission Milestones: An Image and Video Recap
summary: The Artemis II mission launched on April 1, 2026, with a crewed Orion spacecraft for a 10-day voyage around the Moon, splashing down on April 10. The mission included NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4867-4872: Sand Fill In Antofagasta Crater and Finding Our Next Drill Target
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover reached the rim of the Antofagasta crater on Mars, which is 10 meters deep and appears fresh. The rover is now preparing to find its next drilling target.

title: NASA Invites Media to Jordan Artemis Accords Signing Ceremony
summary: The Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan will sign the Artemis Accords during a ceremony at NASA Headquarters in Washington. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman will host the event along with Jordanian and U.S. officials.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from the NASA Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The OIG report warns of significant setbacks in the development of new spacesuits for lunar and ISS missions.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: Seven space missions from four countries are scheduled to launch during the week of April 20 using five different launch vehicles. The missions include GPS, Progress, and Starlink launches.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after a failed launch aboard Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket. The incident marks a setback for the company's satellite broadband ambitions.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission and reused a booster for the first time, just five months after its second launch. This milestone marks a significant step in the development of reusable launch vehicles.

title: Vast unveils Astronaut Flight Suit and revolutionary Large Docking Adapter
summary: Vast introduced an astronaut flight suit and a new Large Docking Adapter, showcasing a blend of fashion and functionality. This innovation supports the development of commercial space stations.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have completed full engine static fires as part of the Artemis program. This marks a significant milestone for the upcoming missions.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew conducted a spacewalk and managed vehicle departures aboard the International Space Station in March. The activities were highlighted in a report by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will debut with a previously flown booster. Six launches are planned globally this week.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). The mission follows Northrop Grumman's recent Cygnus S.S. William McCool completion of CRS NG-23.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. This marks a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned in the context of space industry news, but no specific details are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication reflects on five decades of space exploration and innovation.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, held on June 8-9 in Paris, to share feedback on space sector topics. The event includes an online preparatory forum in April and May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal has been successfully placed in an optimal location. This marks a significant milestone for the navigation system.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission reveals that solar wind structures in the inner corona move 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improvements in attitude control systems for satellites or probes. The title suggests a positive direction in this field.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team tested ultralight graphene aerogels with lasers during ESA's parabolic flight campaign, observing significant propulsion in microgravity. The study, published in Advanced Science, suggests potential for laser-based satellite positioning and solar sail steering.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Lasers could soon steer solar sails and adjust satellite positions in space using graphene, a material that may revolutionize propulsion systems. An experiment simulating space conditions demonstrated graphene's potential for future space missions.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A new silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functionalities, potentially revolutionizing space industry applications. The chip's versatility could enable advancements in satellite and spacecraft systems.

title: Lawmakers Promise to Reject Proposed NASA Cuts—Again
summary: Lawmakers have vowed to reject proposed budget cuts to NASA, arguing that the cuts would hinder the agency's ability to meet the objectives set by President Trump. This marks another instance of congressional pushback against reductions in NASA's funding.

title: Tracking Sats From the Sea
summary: The article discusses how satellites provide domain awareness to protect Navy ships and suggests that the Navy could assist in tracking these satellites. It highlights a potential mutual benefit between the Navy and satellite tracking efforts.

title: ATMOS Space Cargo Raises €25.7M Series A
summary: ATMOS Space Cargo has secured €25.7 million in a Series A funding round, enabling the company to transition to regular operational service. The funds will support the development of ATMOS WORKS and PHOENIX 3, aimed at building a full commercial, institutional, and defense-capable architecture.

title: Payload Field Guide: Golden Dome
summary: Golden Dome, initially announced in January 2025, is an ambitious US missile defense program that has attracted significant industry interest. Companies are actively positioning themselves to compete for contracts related to this program.

title: Opportunities Beyond the Moon Opened by CLPS
summary: NASA plans to launch monthly uncrewed missions to the Moon starting next year under its CLPS program, opening opportunities for various companies beyond just lunar-focused ones.

title: Economics of Orbital Data Centers Report: Part 1
summary: The first part of a four-part report analyzes the economics of orbital data centers. It discusses terrestrial bottlenecks pushing compute capabilities into space.

title: NASA’s 2028 Moon Landing Has A Spacesuit Problem, New Report Says
summary: NASA's planned 2028 Moon landing faces delays due to spacesuit development issues, as reported by the Office of the Inspector General (OIG). The setback threatens the agency's timeline for returning astronauts to the lunar surface.

title: Latvia To Join Artemis Accords Today
summary: Latvia is set to join the Artemis Accords today, marking a significant step in international space cooperation. The move aligns with efforts to transition from symbolic support to active contributions in NASA's Artemis program.

title: SpaceX Won A Mars Mission That Might Get Cancelled
summary: SpaceX has been awarded a contract to launch the ESA Rosalind Franklin rover to Mars as early as late 2028 for $175.7M, but NASA's potential cancellation of its participation in the mission due to budget constraints could impact the project.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Thruster Market with Gauss
summary: Rocket Lab is expanding into the thruster market, aiming to vertically integrate and address scale concerns for customers. The company's CFO highlighted the need for reliable suppliers as early-stage development transitions to large-scale production.

title: US Officials See Proposed European Union Space Act as Complicated Overreach
summary: U.S. officials express concerns over the proposed European Union Space Act, viewing it as a form of complicated overreach. The act aims to create a unified regulatory framework for EU space activities.

title: Australian Airline Jetstar Selects Viasat for IFC for Dreamliner Fleet
summary: Jetstar Airways, an Australian low-cost airline, has chosen Viasat's Amara solution for in-flight connectivity on its Boeing 787 Dreamliner fleet. The Amara system will be installed across 11 Jetstar aircraft.

title: SSC Space and Kuva Space to Collaborate in Nordic Region
summary: SSC Space and Kuva Space have announced a regional collaboration to enhance space-based capabilities across the Nordic region's space value chain. The partnership aims to strengthen various sectors within the space industry.

title: FCC Grants AST SpaceMobile Commercial Authorization for Direct-to-Device Service
summary: The FCC has granted AST SpaceMobile commercial authorization to provide direct-to-device services using spectrum from partner mobile network operators. The approval was announced in an order released on April 21.

title: Eutelsat Signs New Broadcast Deals in Mexico and Caribbean
summary: Eutelsat has signed three new broadcast deals in Mexico and the Caribbean, partnering with Co-op Cable to expand direct-to-home (DTH) and connectivity services.

title: Pentagon to Move Forward On AEP Upgrades for GPS, As DoD Cancels GPS OCX
summary: The Pentagon has decided to cancel the GPS Next Generation Operational Control System (OCX) developed by RTX and instead proceed with upgrades to the existing GPS ground system. Lockheed Martin's Architecture Evolution Plan (AEP) will be the basis for these upgrades.

title: Intellian Details New Terminals for Aviation and WGS Network
summary: Intellian Technologies introduced two new terminals, including its first aviation terminal and a new tactical flyaway terminal. The company is expanding its satellite technology offerings.

title: Eutelsat Retires GEO Satellite 139 West A to Graveyard Orbit
summary: Eutelsat has retired its GEO communications satellite Eutelsat 139 West A after 22 years of service, moving it to a graveyard orbit. The satellite's retirement was announced in a statement following the completion of orbit-raising maneuvers.

title: Air India Chief Digital Officer Talks IFC Roadmap at AIX: Video Interview
summary: Air India's Chief Digital and Technology Officer discussed the airline's In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) roadmap at the Aircraft Interiors Expo (AIX) in Hamburg. The interview was conducted by Via Satellite's Senior Editorial Director Mark Holmes.

title: Airline, OEM, Satellite Players Say IFC Experience is in the ‘Details’
summary: Airlines, OEMs, and satellite providers are collaborating to improve in-flight connectivity (IFC) experiences by addressing challenges and meeting rising customer expectations. This was discussed at the Aircraft Interiors (AIX) event.
